channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Crossing has been moved without the green man lights on one side of road, 6th March
item.description:
When standing in front of pembury tavern and crossing Amhurst road through the road works - the crossing has recently been moved to the right - but the crossing lights for the pembury tavern half of the road from either direction have not been moved in line with the crossing which means all you look at is the crossing light on the far side of the road which turns red and green at different times to the first half of the crossing which is very dangerous. The pedestrian crossing lights and button for the pembury tavern side of the road need to be moved in line with and accessible from the new crossing

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 238a, Dalston Lane, Clapton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo on the corridor on the 5th floor, 6th March
item.description:
Dog poo on the corridor on the 5th floor

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chatham Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike frames, 6th March
item.description:
3 bike frames rusted up and in disrepair have been chained to the Helmsley place bike rack for over 1.5 years now. E8 3SB

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Helmsley Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 6th March
item.description:
Black bags of rubbish left in alleyway

item.title: Discarded Christmas trees, 6th March
item.description:
Discarded Christmas trees left over from Christmas has been left on the street and needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 87, Enid Street, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.title: Abandoned Santander bike, 6th March
item.description:
Side of 103 Datchet Road Please clear a Santander bike

item.title: Huge sequence of deep potholes, 6th March
item.description:
The potholes are getting worse by the day, and this is a busy road and it's getting very difficult to dodge them

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mount Pleasant, East Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flickering light, 6th March
item.description:
The lamp column is flickering on and off
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 66, 6th March
item.description:
Issue was previously reported but marked as fixed when no resurfacing of the road happened. So here it is again. The road surface is quite damaged with significant dips and potholes which leads to severe vibrations in my property whenever one of the C11 bus pass by. The problem is further aggravated when the bus is electric due to the increased weight of the machine. Considering the issue has been ongoing for months now, can you please urgently action a resurfacing of the road to prevent such holes and subsequent vibrations? thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Canfield Gardens, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped waste materials dumped in private carpark, 6th March
item.description:
Today on 6 March at c2.50pm my elderly neighbour said a white transit van unloaded waste in to the property carpark. The dumped materials are at the back of the carpark by the fence and look like dismantled furniture.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fox Hill, Upper Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paint dust from old yellow lines just left in gutter and on pavement, source of microplastic pollution, 6th March
item.description:
Paint is a leading cause of microplastic and heavy metals pollution but paint dust from old yellow lines (scraped up as new lines have been painted) has been left on road and pavement. This could be inhaled by people and wildlife, it may be washed into the water table and enter tap water. It also looks unsightly
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 83, Hayes Lane, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A few yards down Coventry Close from Kilburn High st., 6th March
item.description:
There’s an abundance of rubbish spilled on to the road and pavement by a garage with graffiti.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Coventry Close, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Gulley drain blocked again, large puddle, Valley Rd, Shortlands about 10m from the jct with Shortlands Rd, adjoining maisonettes 7 and 8 of Beech Tree Court, Shortlands Rd., 6th March
item.description:
Gulley drain blocked again, large puddle, Valley Rd, Shortlands about 10m from the jct with Shortlands Rd, adjoining maisonettes 7 and 8 of Beech Tree Court, Shortlands Rd. This is a recurring problem each time it rains. Seen at about 4pm Friday 6/3/26. This is a low spot by the ascending land of Shortlands Rd, Mays Hill Rd and Valley Road. I see two drains marked on the OS map, but a remember only one.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Valley Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Top of Cambridge Avenue (Kilburn High Road end), 6th March
item.description:
There is a fenced off area joining a purpose-built, now unused building, filled to the brim with rubbish, in an area measuring approximately 6 metres. A previous complaint has been filed and nothing has been done. It is a health hazard and an eye sore.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cambridge Avenue, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street cleaning - Grass verges / Shrub beds - Littering

item.title: Trip hazard paving slab(s) in Newlands Park, Penge, about 10m from the junction with Lennard Rd, by the second tree from the corner., 6th March
item.description:
Trip hazard paving slab(s) in Newlands Park, Penge, about 10m from the junction with Lennard Rd, by the second tree from the corner. There are large tree roots here gradually lifting the tarmac and pink slabs over the last three years or more. However I noticed this particular tilted slab today 6/3/26, after the adjoining block of flats residential fence was replaced in the last week or two. Perhaps replacing the fence with new wooden slatted panels, concrete posts and concrete toe-boards, has tilted this slab dangerously to trip over. Please also look at the circa 20m long paved / tarmacked footway here in general, too. It is very uneven, probably from adjoining tree root growth. I have great difficulty walking over it especially at night when it is difficult to see and avoid the raised lumps. Please do not fell the trees but instead smooth out the footway. I can see that generally raising the footway might also require raising the carriageway edge to avoid drivers opening car doors into the footway surface. This is a busy location next to the North side of Penge East Station. The footbridge also provides a busy location for the public to cross between the two sides of the railway line.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Newlands Park, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Armchair still uncollected, 6th March
item.description:
Armchair still uncollected over a month now on Albatross Way bike rack. Please remove !

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albatross Way, Rotherhithe, Greater London

item.title: Manhole cover clanging 24/7, 6th March
item.description:
The noise from the manhole cover clangs 24/7. Where Arbinger Grove meets Evelyn Street
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prince Street, New Cross, Greater London

item.title: 4 bags of rubbish / old clothing have been dumped on the pavement, 6th March
item.description:
Four sacks of rubbish / old clothing were dumped on the pavement of Queenscroft Road (about 100 yards from the junction with Eltham Hill) on the East side of the road. I noticed them on 6th March (although they could have been there late the day before). Similar rubbish was dumped nearby a few days ago but that has been removed.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queenscroft Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet
